stem:
The table below shows selected values of the linear function $f$.

| $x$ | $f(x)$ |
|-----|--------|
| $0$ | $7$    |
| $2$ | $13$   |
| $4$ | $19$   |

What is the slope of the graph of $f$ in the $xy$-plane?
explanation:
The slope of a linear function is the rate of change, calculated as $m = \dfrac{\Delta f(x)}{\Delta x}$. Using any two rows from the table:

Using $x = 0$ and $x = 2$:
$$m = \frac{13 - 7}{2 - 0} = \frac{6}{2} = 3$$

This can be verified with the other pair: using $x = 2$ and $x = 4$:
$$m = \frac{19 - 13}{4 - 2} = \frac{6}{2} = 3$$

The slope is $3$.

distractorRationale.A: Reads the y-intercept $f(0) = 7$ as the slope instead of computing the rate of change between two points.
distractorRationale.B: Inverts the slope formula, computing run divided by rise: $\frac{2}{6} = \frac{1}{3}$ instead of $\frac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}} = \frac{6}{2} = 3$.
distractorRationale.D: Computes only the rise (the change in $f(x)$) without dividing by the run: $13 - 7 = 6$, omitting the division by $\Delta x = 2$.
